title: Residential Lease Agreement
description: A standard residential lease contract setting term duration, rent payments, security deposits, utility guidelines, and maintenance expectations.
whatsCovered: Definition of premises, landlord, and tenant parties
Lease term duration (fixed term or month-to-month)
Monthly rent rate, due dates, and late payment penalties
Security deposit amount and terms of return
Landlord maintenance obligations and tenant rules
whenToUse: Use this agreement when renting out a residential house, apartment, condo, or bedroom to a tenant.

fields.label: Landlord Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 202 Real Estate Rd, Miami, FL 33130

fields.label: Property/Premises Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 789 Ocean Blvd, Apt 5A, Miami, FL 33139

clauses.heading: RESIDENTIAL LEASE AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This Residential Lease Agreement (the "Lease") is entered into by and between {landlord_name}, located at {landlord_address} ("Landlord"), and {tenant_name} ("Tenant"). Landlord leases to Tenant, and Tenant rents from Landlord, the residential premises located at: {premises_address} (the "Premises").

clauses.heading: 1. Term
clauses.body: The Lease shall commence on {lease_start} and shall expire on {lease_end} (the "Term"). Upon expiration, the Lease shall not automatically renew unless a written extension is executed by both Parties.

clauses.heading: 2. Rent
clauses.body: Tenant agrees to pay Landlord a monthly rent of {monthly_rent}, due in advance on the 1st day of each calendar month. Rent shall be paid via electronic bank transfer or check.

clauses.heading: 3. Security Deposit
clauses.body: Upon signing this Lease, Tenant shall pay Landlord a security deposit of {security_deposit}. Landlord shall hold the security deposit in a separate interest-bearing account and return it to Tenant, minus any allowable deductions for damages beyond normal wear and tear, within 30 days of the Tenant vacating the Premises.

clauses.heading: 4. Pets Policy
clauses.body: Tenant shall be permitted to keep up to two common household pets (dogs/cats under 40 lbs) on the Premises, subject to a monthly non-refundable pet fee of $50.00.

clauses.heading: 5. Subleasing Restriction
clauses.body: Tenant shall not sublease, assign, or transfer any part of the Premises without Landlord's prior written approval, which may be withheld at Landlord's sole discretion.

clauses.heading: 6. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Lease shall be governed and interpreted according to the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}, including all tenant-landlord protection statutes.

faq.question: Can the landlord enter the apartment at any time?
faq.answer: No. Most jurisdictions require landlords to give at least 24 or 48 hours' written notice before entering the property, except in case of emergencies.

faq.question: What is security deposit protection?
faq.answer: Landlords are legally obligated to return your deposit after you move out, unless there is actual damage to the premises that exceeds normal wear and tear.
